title: vaccinium erythrococcum
intro: Vaccinium erythrococcum, also known as the red-berried cranberry or lingonberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to boreal forests in North America. The medicinal properties of Vaccinium erythrococcum include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing the risk of urinary tract infections and improving cardiovascular health. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Vaccinium erythrococcum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
